content.problemStatement:
For two strings AAA and BBB with the same length NNN, we will say that they are equivalent if the following rule holds: For each pair of integers lll and rrr (1≤l≤r≤N1 \le l \le r \le N1≤l≤r≤N), the substring of AAA between the lll-th and rrr-th character (inclusive) is a palindrome if and only if the substring of BBB between the lll-th and rrr-th character (inclusive) is a palindrome.

You are given a string SSS with length NNN. Find the number of sets P⊂{1,2,…,N}P \subset \{1, 2, \ldots, N\}P⊂{1,2,…,N} such that there is a character ccc and a string XXX which is equivalent to SSS and satisfies Xp=cX_p = cXp​=c for each p∈Pp \in Pp∈P. Since the answer may be large, compute it modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase Latin letters
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single string SSS with length NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of subsets of characters that can be equal to each other in some string equivalent to SSS, modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
